title: Duplicate-kind state_derivation.rollups entries are schema-expressible and make currentState diverge set-level from the invariant evaluation
description: config.schema.json's state_derivation.rollups array carries no uniqueItems or per-kind uniqueness constraint, and resolveStateDerivation returns the raw config, so a config declaring the same kind in two rollup entries loads without complaint. The two consumers then disagree structurally: currentState's milestones walk visits items once PER ROLLUP ENTRY (duplicating milestones[] entries and drift-head divergence collection), while evaluateConfigInvariants builds rollupByKind as a last-wins Map and visits items ONCE. Empirically constructed during the drift-surfacing re-audit: two entries for the milestone kind yielded an 8-entry drift head against the invariant evaluation's 4 issues -- set-level divergence, not mere ordering -- and milestones[] itself duplicates under that shape. The stock catalog never ships duplicate kinds, so no shipped configuration manifests it; the divergence is reachable by any hand-authored or amended config. The ordering guarantee the drift head documents is void under this degenerate shape.

evidence.reference: the rollups array schema declares items but no uniqueItems and no kind-uniqueness constraint

the milestones walk iterates sd.rollups outer (per-entry item visitation); evaluateConfigInvariants' derived-status branch builds a last-wins Map keyed by kind (single visitation) -- two different duplicate-kind semantics on the same data
the drift-surfacing grounding; the re-audit of its ordering fix constructed the duplicate-kind case empirically (8-vs-4 entries) and verified the divergence predates the fix

impact: A config that declares the same rollup kind twice -- an easy hand-authoring or amend mistake with no write-time or validate-time signal -- silently produces duplicated milestones entries and a drift head that disagrees set-level with the validate surface, corroding exactly the single-verdict property the derived-status machinery exists to guarantee.
proposed_resolution: Constrain the vocabulary at its gates rather than reconciling the walks: reject a duplicate rollup kind where config is validated (a validateContext config-shape issue naming the duplicated kind, plus the amend/write path refusing the introduction), making the divergent-walk shapes unreachable through sanctioned writes. The two consumers' duplicate-kind semantics then need no alignment; a config-schema uniqueness expression (or engine-side check where JSON Schema cannot express per-field uniqueness) carries the constraint dual-surface.
